description: Remove unreachable response.ok check after fetchFromPage with redirect:follow in hackernews-api.ts.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/hackernews && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove dead response.ok check after fetchFromPage in hackernews-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/hackernews/src/hackernews-api.ts, a POST with redirect:'follow' calls fetchFromPage which auto-throws on non-ok. The subsequent check `if (!response.ok && response.status !== 302 && response.status !== 301)` at around line 222 is unreachable — fetchFromPage already threw. Remove the dead check and its error handling block.
acceptanceCriteria: The `if (!response.ok && response.status !== 302 && response.status !== 301)` block is removed from hackernews-api.ts
The function still returns successfully after a redirect-followed response
